name: always_shareable
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_shareable(os: &OS, proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_shareable(proc_ptr, va_range) == true,
{
}
reason: If every valid range is always shareable, the shareability check is vacuous and provides no protection against unsafe sharing.

name: never_shareable
code:
proof fn phi_2_never_shareable(os: &OS, proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_shareable(proc_ptr, va_range) == false,
{
}
reason: If no range is ever shareable, the sharing mechanism is completely broken and the feature is dead code.

name: proc_independent_shareable
code:
proof fn phi_3_proc_independent_shareable(os: &OS, proc1: ProcPtr, proc2: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc1),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc2),
        va_range.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_shareable(proc1, va_range) == os.address_space_range_shareable(proc2, va_range),
{
}
reason: If shareability is independent of which process is targeted, the spec ignores per-process address spaces and reference counts, violating process isolation.

name: shareable_implies_empty_range
code:
proof fn phi_4_shareable_implies_empty_range(os: &OS, proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        va_range.wf(),
        os.address_space_range_shareable(proc_ptr, va_range),
    ensures
        va_range.len == 0usize,
{
}
reason: If only zero-length ranges can be shareable, the sharing feature is useless since no actual pages can ever be shared.

name: range_independent_shareable
code:
proof fn phi_5_range_independent_shareable(os: &OS, proc_ptr: ProcPtr, va_range1: &VaRange4K, va_range2: &VaRange4K)
    requires
        os.wf(),
        os.proc_dom().contains(proc_ptr),
        va_range1.wf(),
        va_range2.wf(),
    ensures
        os.address_space_range_shareable(proc_ptr, va_range1) == os.address_space_range_shareable(proc_ptr, va_range2),
{
}
reason: If shareability is independent of the specific VA range, the per-page mapping and reference-count checks are meaningless and any range behaves identically.
